title: Spatial Workshop - Jupyter Notebook Login
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2huan","text":"Log in instructions for the URMC Genomics Research Center summer spatial transcriptomics workshop.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: If you have access to Bluehive, please log in using the "Logging in via Bluehive" method. Otherwise, please log in via Open On-Demand. Jupyter Notebook is a common development environment for working within Python. For the purposes of this workshop, we've prepared a setting in which all of your required packages are already installed. This guide is intended to get you loaded and ready for the workshop.
Set the partition to interactive, CPU's to 2, Memory to 8GB.
Once the session has spawned, select and open the notebook within Jupyter
Copy the blank notebook to your home directory wget https://grcweb.circ.rochester.edu/pickup/250728155436-17128/Spatial_template_blank.ipynb
Change the Kernal to "clong_squidpy (3.10.17)" Within the drop down menu
Log into Bluehive via ssh or an interactive session. This is only a temporary session for the purposes of copying the notebook.
Select "2" for number of cores, "Extreme" for the data size, and 6 hours for the number of hours. Click Launch
Once the session starts, click into the host
Copy the Kernel from our GRC_Workshop folder cp -R /mnt/nfs/GRC/GRC_Workshop/Spatial_pt2/Spatial_Kernel ~/.local/share/jupyter/kernels/
Log into the terminal using your URMC-AD login
Run the following within the command prompt mkdir -p ~/.local/share/jupyter/kernels
Go back to your session window and press the Jupyter Lab button.

To make sure you have the right Kernel loaded, check the top right. As a test, you can type in "import squidpy as sq" and press the play button. You should get some warnings, but this code block should otherwise finish.
Copy the Notebook template from our GRC_Workshop folder as well cp -R /mnt/nfs/GRC/GRC_Workshop/Spatial_pt2/Spatial_template_blank.ipynb ~/
creator: Cameron Baker
